item.title: Storage duration for an object in C++
item.description: Storage duration defines when and for how long an object will exist in the program life cycle.

item.title: Static storage duration of objects in C++
item.description: An object having static storage duration means that its life time would be throughout the entire program.

item.title: External Linkage Property for Identifiers in C++
item.description: External linkage is a property of an identifier that makes it visible to other translation units.

item.title: Internal Linkage Property for Identifiers in C++
item.description: Internal linkage property for an identifier makes it visible to a single translation unit and not accessible from other translation units.

item.title: Linkage
item.description: Linkage of objects in C++. Linkage determines if declaration of the identifier in different scopes refers to the same object or function.

item.title: Creating namespaces in C++
item.description: Creating namespaces in C++. We can use namespace keyword followed by name and block containing declarations and definitions.

item.title: String view modification functions
item.description: String view provides remove_suffix and remove_prefix functions.

item.title: std::string_view in C++
item.description: String view in C++. String views are readonly access to strings.

item.title: String literals in C++
item.description: C++ has C-style string literals by default.
